title: Clear A Flashing ESTOP Condition At The Operator Station
summary: Clear a documented flashing ESTOP condition at the operator station by pulling out the ESTOP and pressing RESET on the HMI screen to clear faults before continuing startup.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when the operator station control panel shows a flashing ESTOP during operator station startup or readiness checks.
not_for: Do not use this procedure for faults other than the documented flashing ESTOP condition.
Do not use this procedure to perform additional fault-clearing actions not described in the source.
Do not continue with normal startup actions until the flashing ESTOP condition has cleared.

responsibilities: Observe the operator station control panel for a flashing ESTOP condition.
Pull out the ESTOP if it is flashing.
Press RESET on the HMI screen to clear faults.
Verify the flashing ESTOP condition has cleared before continuing.

safety_notes: Use only the documented ESTOP pull-out and HMI RESET actions supported by the source.
The source does not provide additional recovery actions if faults do not clear after RESET.
Do not invent additional fault-clearing, bypass, or override actions beyond the documented procedure.

access_required: Access to the operator station control panel
Access to the operator station HMI screen
system_preconditions: A flashing ESTOP condition is present at the operator station control panel.

visual_references.description: System screen artifact from page 82 associated with stopping and startup procedures; relevant to the documented flashing ESTOP and RESET instruction.
Operator station control panel reference showing the primary operator interface context.
Operator station HMI view showing a RESET control in a fault recovery context.
Operator station HMI screen reference showing operator station HMI context.

steps.title: Observe the operator station for a flashing ESTOP
steps.purpose: Confirm whether the documented ESTOP recovery condition is present before taking action.
steps.instruction: At the operator station control panel, observe whether the ESTOP is flashing.
steps.expected_result: The operator determines whether the ESTOP is flashing.
steps.healthy_condition: The ESTOP is not flashing, or the flashing condition is positively identified for recovery.
steps.failure_condition: The ESTOP condition cannot be confirmed or remains flashing and requires the documented reset actions.

actions.label: Check ESTOP status
actions.exact_action: At the operator station control panel, observe whether the ESTOP is flashing.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station control panel context for locating the operator controls.
System/startup screen context associated with the page containing the flashing ESTOP instruction.

stop_or_escalate_if: The ESTOP condition cannot be determined from the operator station control panel.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: At the operator station control panel, if the ESTOP is flashing, pull out the ESTOP and press RESET on the HMI screen to clear any faults.

steps.title: Pull out the flashing ESTOP
steps.purpose: Reset the ESTOP device when the documented flashing condition is present.
steps.instruction: If the ESTOP is flashing, pull out the ESTOP.
steps.expected_result: The ESTOP is physically reset to the pulled-out position.
steps.healthy_condition: The ESTOP has been pulled out as instructed.
steps.failure_condition: The ESTOP remains in the fault condition or cannot be reset by pulling it out.

actions.label: Reset ESTOP
actions.exact_action: If the ESTOP is flashing, pull out the ESTOP.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station control panel area for the ESTOP location.

stop_or_escalate_if: The ESTOP cannot be pulled out or does not reset physically.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: At the operator station control panel, if the ESTOP is flashing, pull out the ESTOP and press RESET on the HMI screen to clear any faults.

steps.title: Press RESET on the HMI to clear faults
steps.purpose: Clear any faults associated with the flashing ESTOP condition using the documented HMI control.
steps.instruction: On the HMI screen, press RESET to clear any faults.
steps.expected_result: Any faults addressed by the documented RESET action are cleared.
steps.healthy_condition: The HMI RESET action completes and the flashing ESTOP-related faults are cleared.
steps.failure_condition: Faults do not clear after pressing RESET.

actions.label: Press RESET
actions.exact_action: On the HMI screen, press RESET to clear any faults.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System/startup screen context associated with the flashing ESTOP and RESET instruction.
RESET control shown on the operator station HMI in a fault recovery context.
General operator station HMI screen context.

stop_or_escalate_if: Faults do not clear after pressing RESET.
Additional recovery steps are needed but are not provided by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: At the operator station control panel, if the ESTOP is flashing, pull out the ESTOP and press RESET on the HMI screen to clear any faults.
Artifact appears on page 84 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_5_7_1_non_recoverable_fault_operator_station_motors.

steps.title: Verify the flashing ESTOP condition has cleared
steps.purpose: Confirm the documented recovery succeeded before resuming normal startup actions.
steps.instruction: Verify the flashing ESTOP condition has cleared before continuing with normal startup actions.
steps.expected_result: The flashing ESTOP condition is no longer present and startup can continue.
steps.healthy_condition: The ESTOP is no longer flashing.
steps.failure_condition: The ESTOP continues flashing or associated faults remain uncleared.

actions.label: Confirm ESTOP cleared
actions.exact_action: Verify the flashing ESTOP condition has cleared before continuing with normal startup actions.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station control panel to confirm the ESTOP is no longer flashing.
Startup procedure context for continuing only after the ESTOP condition is cleared.

stop_or_escalate_if: The ESTOP continues flashing after the documented reset actions.
Faults remain after pressing RESET and no further source-supported steps are available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: At the operator station control panel, if the ESTOP is flashing, pull out the ESTOP and press RESET on the HMI screen to clear any faults.

success_criteria: The ESTOP has been pulled out.
RESET has been pressed on the HMI screen.
Any faults addressed by the documented RESET action are cleared.
The flashing ESTOP condition is no longer present and normal startup can continue.
healthy_conditions: The ESTOP is not flashing after the reset procedure.
The operator station is ready to continue with normal startup actions.
failure_conditions: The ESTOP cannot be reset by pulling it out.
Faults do not clear after pressing RESET.
The flashing ESTOP condition remains present after the documented actions.
The source provides no additional recovery steps for unresolved faults.
escalation_guidance: If faults do not clear after pressing RESET, stop at the documented boundary because the source does not provide additional steps.
If the flashing ESTOP condition remains after completing the documented actions, do not invent further recovery actions; seek SME or site-specific guidance outside this source.

missing_details: The source does not provide additional troubleshooting or escalation steps if faults do not clear after pressing RESET.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.

The source does not identify a specific HMI screen name for the RESET action in this procedure.
